vinīta 1〉 adj. s. u. 1. nī mit vi.
— 2〉 m.
a〉 Kaufmann, Krämer [Page6-1095]  H. an. 3,299 .  MED. t. 154 .
— b〉 Artemisia indica (damanaka)  RĀJAN. _im_ ŚKDR. 
— c〉 N. pr. eines Sohnes des Pulastya  VĀYU-P. _in_ VP. 83, N. 5 .

results.rendered_html:
vinīta p. p.
1 Taken away, removed.
2 Well-trained, educated, disciplined.
3 Refined, well-behaved.
4 Modest, humble, meek, gentle.
5 Decent, decorous, gentlemanly.
6 Sent away, dismissed.
7 Tamed, broken in.
8 Plain, simple, (as a dress).
9 Having the passions under control, selfsubdued.
10 Chastised, punished.
11 Tractable, governable.
12 Lovely, handsome. (See nī with vi also).
— taḥ
1 A trained horse.
2 A trader.
Comp.
— ātman a. humble, lowly.
